'''Ben Condon''' (born 13 July 2000) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] in the [[NRL]].

== Background ==
Born in [[Roma, Queensland]], Condon played his junior rugby league for the [[Wallumbilla]]-[[Surat, Queensland|Surat]] Red Bulls.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.dalbyherald.com.au/news/roma-native-ready-to-represent-his-state/3434949/|title=Roma native ready to represent his state|date=6 June 2018|website=Dalby Herald}}</ref>

He attended [[Rockhampton Grammar School]] before being signed by the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] in 2017.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cairnspost.com.au/sport/local-sport/nq-cowboys-ben-condon-ready-for-intrust-super-cup-debut/news-story/349fc8f7a5e22f847941bc3f42a2ae6a|title=NQ Cowboys' Ben Condon ready for Intrust Super Cup debut|date=15 March 2020|website=Cairns Post}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2017-05-26/young-rugby-league-players-focus-on-long-term-careers/8547194|title=Young aspiring rugby league players encouraged to have long term career plans|date=26 May 2017|website=ABC}}</ref>

== Playing career ==
===Early career===
In 2016, Condon played for the [[Central Queensland Capras]] [[Cyril Connell Cup]] team before moving up to their [[Mal Meninga Cup]] team in 2017.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2016/04/13/capras-easts-aim-for-final-round-win/|title=Capras, Easts aim for final round win|date=13 April 2016|website=QRL}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.capras.com.au/2017/03/cq-capras-mal-meninga-u18s-round-1-squad-announced/|title=CQ Capras Mal Meninga U18's Round 1 Squad Announced|date=4 March 2017|website=CQ Capras}}</ref> In 2018, Condon moved to [[Townsville, Queensland]] and joined the [[Townsville Blackhawks]]. After starting the year with the [[Mal Meninga Cup]] side, he later progressed to their [[Hastings Deering Colts]] team, scoring a try in their Grand Final loss to the [[Norths Devils]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://blackhawkstsv.com.au/2018/09/27/mendi-blackhawks-u20s/|title=U20s Grand Final v Norths Devils|date=27 September 2018|website=Townsville Blackhawks}}</ref>

In 2019, he spent the season with the Blackhawks' under-20 side and represented the [[Queensland under-20 rugby league team|Queensland under-20]] team.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2019/07/09/seven-cowboys-in-queensland-u20s-squad/|title=Seven Cowboys in Queensland u20s squad|date=9 July 2019|website=NQ Cowboys}}</ref> On 24 September 2019, Condon signed a development contract with the [[North Queensland Cowboys]], joining their [[NRL]] squad.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2019/09/24/cowboys-promote-five-young-guns/|title=Cowboys promote five young guns to NRL squad for 2020|date=24 September 2019|website=NQ Cowboys}}</ref>

===2020===
In 2020, Condon started the season playing for the [[Northern Pride RLFC|Northern Pride]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2020/08/07/cowboy-condon-is-ready-for-his-next-chance/|title=Cowboy Condon is ready for his next chance|date=7 August 2020|website=QRL}}</ref> However the [[Queensland Cup]] competition was cancelled after just one round due to the [[COVID-19 pandemic in Australia|COVID-19 pandemic]].

Condon made his made his NRL debut for [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Queensland]] in Round 19 of the [[2020 NRL season]], against [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2020/09/15/cowboys-team-list-round-19-v-penrith/|title=Cowboys team list: Round 19 v Penrith|date=15 September 2020|website=NQ Cowboys}}</ref>

===2021 & 2022===
Condon made 11 appearances for North Queensland in the [[2021 NRL season]] as the club finished 15th on the table.

===2023===
On 19 October 2022, Condon signed a three-year deal to join [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly-Warringah]] starting in 2023.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-transfer-centre-squads-broncos-storm-dolphins-dragons-rabbitohs-bulldogs-player-movement-contracts-signings/news-story/3927328fea7220bdaa255b24983fd028/|title=Manly sign Cowboys wantaway; Dragons release Burgess in big shake-up: Transfer Centre|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref> Condon was limited to just four games with Manly in the [[2023 NRL season]] as the club finished 12th on the table and missed the finals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-manly-sea-eagles-season-review-anthony-seibold-josh-schuster-salary-tom-trbojevic-positional-changes-tolu-koula-daly-cherryevans-news/news-story/3deeeb674db182b6335fd5a9b2fc4597/|title=The $800k call Seibold must make as Turbo hits NRL crossroads: Sea Eagles Brutal Review|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

===2024===
On 11 October 2024 it was announced that Condon had signed a 2-year deal for the [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Super League]]<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=11 Oct 2024|title=Leigh sign Australian back rower Condon for 2025|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cz6w2p167j1o}}</ref> On 15 December it was announced that the deal fell through because Condon failed his medical.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-12-15 |title=Leigh Leopards confirm reason behind collapse of overseas star's move ahead of 2025 |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leigh-leopards-confirm-reason-behind-collapse-of-overseas-stars-move-ahead-of-2025 |access-date=2024-12-21 |website=Love Rugby League |language=en}}</ref>

=== 2025 ===
On 13 January, it was announced that Condon had signed on to play with the [[Central Queensland Capras]] in the QLD cup.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chalk |first=Ethan Lee |date=2025-01-14 |title=Ex-Manly forward links up with new team for 2025 |url=https://www.zerotackle.com/ex-manly-forward-links-up-with-new-team-for-2025-218161/ |access-date=2025-01-14 |website=Zero Tackle |language=en-US}}</ref>

On 27 June 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship]] for the remainder of the 2025 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=27 Jun 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Widnes land deal for ex-Leigh target after Super League move collapse|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/widnes-land-deal-for-ex-leigh-target-after-super-league-move-collapse}}</ref>

On 14 October 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]] for 2026 on a two-year deal<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=14 Oct 2025|accessdate=15 Oct 2025|author=Callum Walker|title=Ben Condon makes Catalans Dragons switch|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ben-condon-makes-surprise-super-league-switch/}}</ref>
